name: Orange, Grapefruit Marmalade
recipeInstructions:
1. Quarter the grapefruit and remove the rind. Discard the seeds and separate the pulp from the membranes.
2. Cut the oranges into quarters; remove the rind and add the orange pulp to the grapefruit. Scrape the pith from the orange rinds.
3. Cut the rinds into fine strips. Repeat for the grapefruit rinds. Quarter the limes or lemons and slice them vertically into thin strips, including the rind. Remove any seeds.
4. Combine all the pulp and rinds in a large pot with water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 to 30 minutes.
5. Remove from heat and let stand for 12 hours or overnight.
6. Add sugar and bring mixture to a rapid boil. Cook for 45 to 60 minutes until the liquid reaches jelly stage (220-225°F). Stir frequently to avoid scorching as water evaporates.
7. Optional: Add Cointreau or any orange liqueur.
8. Ladle into scalded jars. Yields about three pints.
